instructor.name: Yan Li
instructor.office_hours: Thursday 1:30 PM – 2:30 PM. Additional office hours will be provided in exam week.

chunks.title: General Principles on Course Policy
chunks.content:
Before you proceed, here I would like to explain to you my principles on the grading and various policies of the course.
* Grades is no more important than your actual understanding. Our purpose of discussion to promote understanding of probability and statistical principles, instead of memorizing various formulas and procedures. I do not care about grades that much, and I assure you that if you understand our in-class discussions, your grades will reflect that.
* We are here to help you learn. We are more than happy to help you understand things that are not clear to you. I will be happy to hop on a 10 PM call (and I said this because I did exactly that) with you if you really find something difficult to understand. We hope you understand that we are NOT score/grades merchant. We are not excited in grades/points negotiation. Especially when you never come to office hours.
* Academic integrity is very, very important. It has absolutely nothing to do with specific school honor code. I completely understand you might have certain preference or dislike of certain academic subjects. But what you do in your academic work is a reflection of you as a person. There is not smartness of finding the best way to cheat. The bottom line is that, if you know you are cheating in some way, please trust me that I can also sense what you are doing.

chunks.title: Email Policy
chunks.content:
Please kindly do not email me or the TA on the following matter:
* Please, please do not email asking me to check your solution for a homework question. If you have doubts on your solution, you are more than welcome to come to my or TA's office hours.
* Please do not submit your late homework assignment by emailing it to me or TA. You can directly submit it on Canvas. For other special situation related to homework, please email TA while copying me on the email.
* Please, do not disappear for the exam, and later ask for make-up exams by emailing me. If in special sitution that you will miss an exam, please make sure to email me before hand. If you decide to miss exam without notice, I respect it by assuming that you are ready to take the responsibility.
For many of the items listed above, I have replied at least a hundred emails of them in a single semester.
For most of the questions in these emails, we could have discussed in GroupChat or office hours or in the class.
The above email policy is strict. Except for exceptional cases, I will not respond if your email falls into the above category.

chunks.title: Course Description
chunks.content: Development and application of fundamental deterministic optimization models, including linear optimization, integer optimization, and nonlinear optimization; algorithms and analytical methods for linear optimization; computer tools for modeling and solving real-life problems.

chunks.title: Course Objectives
Prerequisites
chunks.content: MATH 304 Linear Algebra (or equivalent, e.g., Math 323)

chunks.title: Computer Software
chunks.content: Python will be our main programming language as it is open-sourced and widely used for both academic and industry use.

chunks.title: Textbook & Other Course Content
chunks.content:
Winston, W. L., M. Venkataramanan, and J. B. Goldberg. Introduction to mathematical programming: operations research.
* Lecture Notes: Lecture slides/notes and material will be provided after each lecture. These will be based on the handbook resource from Dr. Sergiy Butenko. (I tend to believe they might be more useful than the textbook itself).

chunks.title: Annoucement
chunks.content: The course website will be maintained through canvas. This site will contain announcements and other information concerning the course. In addition, it will be used to distribute class materials (homework assignments, notes), grades and to facilitate communication between the instructor and students. Please check the web site daily any information posted on it will be as valid as if it was mentioned in class. Students are recommended to carefully read all the announcements in its entirety.

chunks.title: Course Topics
chunks.content:
1. Part I. Linear Optimization: Model and Algorithms
* Linear optimization modeling
* Algorithms for linear optimization
* Computing Software
2. Part II. Linear Optimization: In-depth Analysis
* Duality and sensitivity analysis
3. Part III. Extension of Linear Optimization
(Depending on our in-class discussion & progress, we will decide whether we will talk about A or B in detail)
* Introduction to integer optimization
* Graphs and network optimization (A)
* Introduction to nonlinear optimization (B)
* Computing Software

chunks.title: Grading
chunks.content:
Grade Constitution. Final grade consists of the following:
* Exam 1: 30%
Tentative date: Feb 17.
* Exam 2: 30%
Tentative date: Mar 24.
* Exam 3: 30%
Tentative date: April 28.
Confirmed exam date will be released on Canvas at least 1 week in advance.
* Homework: 10%
Any two homeworks will be separated by at least 7 days.
* Additional bonus: up to 6 in accumulation
These will be given in the form of bonus questions in (1) homework & exam, and (2) in-class discussion participation. The value of each question will be stated explicitly. For instance, "By completing this bonus question, you will earn 1 bonus point toward your accumulated bonus". Note that if you have earned more than 6 bonus points during the semester, the total bonus will still be 6 points.
* Attendance: we might have up to 3 attendance on random dates to be included in the bonus point.
In summary, the total raw grade is:
Final grade = Exam 1.0.3 + Exam 20.3 + Exam 3.0.3 + Homework. 0.1
+ min {accumulated bonus points, 6} .
That is, the maximum possible points of your raw grade is 110.
Letter Grade Minimum Averages Required (Tentative): A: 90; B: 80; C: 70; D: 60; F: <60.
I would like to reserve the right to adjust the Minimum Average to avoid certain extreme cases.

chunks.title: Exam Policy
chunks.content:
* Exames are in-class, closed book and notes. One-page (double-sided) cheat sheet is allowed. Most exam questions will be based on homework and lecture notes. No electronic device except basic calculator is allowed in the test.
* Make-up exams are permitted only when the situation is in accordance with the (see Rule 7 at http://student-rules.tamu.edu). If you expect you will miss certain exams, please make sure you inform me before before the exam.
We will not accommodate make-up exams for request made after exams. There will absolutely be no exam-retake where you take any exam twice.

chunks.title: Homework Policy
chunks.content:
It is allowed to work together on homework assignments, but your handed-in solutions should be personal and show individual effort (not identical to the others' assignments nor the previous solutions). For the regular assignments, please upload an electronic pdf for your solution, you can also scan it and upload the scanned pdf.
We allow a grace period of 2 days, so that you can submit the late homework within 2 days after the due date. The late penalty will be 20% of the homework grade.
If under exceptional reasons you expect you will be more than 2 days late, please let us know before hand. If we do not receive prior notice from you with a valid reason, unfortunately we will not accept any submission 2 days after deadline.
Communication of homework should be sent to TA while cc'ing me. Please do not send your late homework directly to us.

chunks.title: GPT Policy
chunks.content: Please refrain from using ChatGPT in your homework. There is no penalty if you do that in terms of your homework grade, but the lact of understanding by relying on this technology will be gauranteed to reflected in the exam.

chunks.title: Grade Check & Regrade Request
chunks.content: You have one week from the day we return homework or exams for considering re-grading. We reserve the right to re-grade the entire homework or exam. So please keep in mind, you may lose more points than you gain when we re-grade your homework or exam.

chunks.title: GroupMe
chunks.content:
We highly encourage posting questions and helping answer others' questions by joining the GroupMe chat:
https://groupme.com/join_group/112426162/3LUpxdp4
* The most active member (defined by answering the most questions in the chat) will receive 2 bonus points directly added to total grades (see details in Grading policy below).
* We will check messages on weekly basis. During exam week it will be monitored on daily basis.
* Please post any technical questions in the group chat instead of directly messaging me. In the previous semester I have responded to too many technical questions on homework and exam prep via direct messaging on Groupchat.
* For direct messages, I will only answer to special situations and logistical questions.

warnings: Credit hours not specified in course_metadata
TA office hours and location not specified
Schedule has only tentative exam dates, no detailed weekly topics with dates or specific assignment due dates (other than homework separation rule)
